p.seg: Let present and future know that I, Simon of Lindsay, have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm to Patrick my man and [in return for] his homage and service in my land at Mow, which he has had from the gift of my mother, six acres of land, and one acre of meadow, and one and a half acres of land in toft and croft, and one acre of meadow of the the land nearest ‘Erdeburesburne’, and ‘Subchestres[?burne]’ . ?two acres . and upon ‘Selestede Adam’ two and a half acres; for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage freely and quitely, fully and honourably, also with all common easements of my land and common easements which my men in my mother’s land [can] and ought to have; by paying me and my heirs one pound of cumin or three pence at the feast of St James in return for all services pertaining to me or my heirs, and by doing such forinsec service on my land as pertains to such an amount of land; I therefore will that he should give four pence [to me and] my heirs for his fine, and six pence for his aid.
